Blueprints.description: A list of available blueprints for Data Prepper.

Service.description: The name of the service that the blueprint is associated with.

UseCase.description: The use case that the blueprint relates to.

NextToken.description: When nextToken is returned, there are more results available. The value of nextToken is a unique pagination token for each page. Make the call again using the returned token to retrieve the next page.

PipelineArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the pipeline in the endpoint connection.

EndpointId.description: The unique identifier of the endpoint in the connection.

Status.description: The current status of the pipeline endpoint connection.

VpcEndpointOwner.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the VPC endpoint used in this connection.

NextToken.description: When NextToken is returned, there are more results available. The value of NextToken is a unique pagination token for each page. Make the call again using the returned token to retrieve the next page.

PipelineArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the pipeline associated with this endpoint.

EndpointId.description: The unique identifier for the pipeline endpoint.

Status.description: The current status of the pipeline endpoint.

VpcId.description: The ID of the VPC where the pipeline endpoint is created.

VpcOptions.description: Configuration options for the VPC endpoint, including subnet and security group settings.

SubnetIds.description: A list of subnet IDs where the pipeline endpoint network interfaces are created.

SecurityGroupIds.description: A list of security group IDs that control network access to the pipeline endpoint.

IngestEndpointUrl.description: The URL used to ingest data to the pipeline through the VPC endpoint.

NextToken.description: When nextToken is returned, there are more results available. The value of nextToken is a unique pagination token for each page. Make the call again using the returned token to retrieve the next page.

Pipelines.description: A list of all existing Data Prepper pipelines.

Description.description: A description of why a pipeline has a certain status.

PipelineArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the pipeline.

MinUnits.description: The minimum pipeline capacity, in Ingestion Compute Units (ICUs).

MaxUnits.description: The maximum pipeline capacity, in Ingestion Compute Units (ICUs).

CreatedAt.description: The date and time when the pipeline was created.

LastUpdatedAt.description: The date and time when the pipeline was last updated.

Destinations.description: A list of destinations to which the pipeline writes data.

Tags.description: A list of tags associated with the given pipeline.

Pipeline.description: Container for information about the created pipeline.

PipelineArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the pipeline.

MinUnits.description: The minimum pipeline capacity, in Ingestion Compute Units (ICUs).

MaxUnits.description: The maximum pipeline capacity, in Ingestion Compute Units (ICUs).

StatusReason.description: The reason for the current status of the pipeline.

PipelineConfigurationBody.description: The Data Prepper pipeline configuration in YAML format.

CreatedAt.description: The date and time when the pipeline was created.

LastUpdatedAt.description: The date and time when the pipeline was last updated.

IngestEndpointUrls.description: The ingestion endpoints for the pipeline, which you can send data to.

LogPublishingOptions.description: Key-value pairs that represent log publishing settings.

CloudWatchLogDestination.description: The destination for OpenSearch Ingestion logs sent to Amazon CloudWatch Logs. This parameter is required if IsLoggingEnabled is set to true.

LogGroup.description: The name of the CloudWatch Logs group to send pipeline logs to. You can specify an existing log group or create a new one. For example, /aws/vendedlogs/OpenSearchService/pipelines.

VpcEndpoints.description: The VPC interface endpoints that have access to the pipeline.

BufferOptions.description: Options that specify the configuration of a persistent buffer. To configure how OpenSearch Ingestion encrypts this data, set the EncryptionAtRestOptions. For more information, see Persistent buffering.

EncryptionAtRestOptions.description: Options to control how OpenSearch encrypts buffer data.

KmsKeyArn.description: The ARN of the KMS key used to encrypt buffer data. By default, data is encrypted using an Amazon Web Services owned key.

VpcEndpointService.description: The VPC endpoint service name for the pipeline.

ServiceVpcEndpoints.description: A list of VPC endpoints that OpenSearch Ingestion has created to other Amazon Web Services services.

Destinations.description: Destinations to which the pipeline writes data.

Tags.description: A list of tags associated with the given pipeline.

PipelineRoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role that the pipeline uses to access AWS resources.

ChangeProgressStatuses.description: The current status of the change happening on the pipeline.

StartTime.description: The time at which the configuration change is made on the pipeline.

Status.description: The overall status of the pipeline configuration change.

TotalNumberOfStages.description: The total number of stages required for the pipeline configuration change.

ChangeProgressStages.description: Information about the stages that the pipeline is going through to perform the configuration change.

ResourceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the resource.

Policy.description: The resource-based policy document in JSON format.

Tags.description: A list of tags associated with the given pipeline.

Key.description: The tag key. Tag keys must be unique for the pipeline to which they are attached.

Value.description: The value assigned to the corresponding tag key. Tag values can be null and don't have to be unique in a tag set. For example, you can have a key value pair in a tag set of project : Trinity and cost-center : Trinity
